Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper consistently identifies the enzyme as Pq3-O-UGT2, an important UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase; this expanded enzyme name is faithful.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The enzyme glycosylates ginsenosides Rh2 and F2, fitting ginsenoside glycosylation/triterpenoid saponin biosynthesis as described throughout Results.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results state transfer to the C2-OH of the C3-glucose of Rh2/F2, i.e. 3-O-glycan chain extension; Section 2.8 also shows liquiritin glycosylation as side activity.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Section 2.1 states glucose transfer from UDP-Glc to the C2-OH of the C3-glucose of Rh2 and F2; Section 2.8 states β(1-2) glycosylation of liquiritin C3-O-glucose to liquiritin-2′-O-glucoside.

EC_Number.reason: The paper establishes glycosyltransferase activity but does not explicitly provide the EC number '2.4.1.-'.

Primary_Substrate.reason: UDP-Glc is the donor, and Rh2, F2, and liquiritin are experimentally tested acceptor substrates in Results/Methods.

Primary_Product.reason: Rg3 and Rd are identified as products of Rh2 and F2 glycosylation, and liquiritin-2′-O-glucoside is identified in Section 2.8/Figure 7.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The enzyme directly catalyzes formation of the named products from tested substrates.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The reactions are glycan chain extension/decorative glycosylation of existing glycosides, consistent with a downstream terminal decoration step.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper supports that this is a key product-determining glycosylation for Rh2→Rg3, but it does not experimentally test rate-limiting status in vivo.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results and Table 1 show formation of Rg3 and Rd, higher wild-type catalytic efficiency toward F2 than Rh2, and that engineered variants can shift selectivity toward Rh2.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are present: crystal structures, mutagenesis, enzyme assays, kinetics, docking, HPLC/LC-MS, and NMR for liquiritin product.

Source_Species.reason: The paper itself does not explicitly spell out the source species next to the gene name in the provided text, though 'Pq' commonly refers to Panax quinquefolius and cited prior work likely does so.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Panax quinquefolius is not explicitly stated in the provided paper text for Pq3-O-UGT2, so strict support is lacking.

Applied_Species.reason: Methods state expression in E. coli Rosetta (DE3).

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods explicitly state E. coli Rosetta (DE3), i.e. Escherichia coli.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods explicitly name the strain Rosetta (DE3).

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper states cloning into pET-28a and expression in E. coli, but does not explicitly mention the T7 promoter or inducibility details in the provided text.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results state conserved GT-B fold and PSPG motif; the enzyme is described as a plant UGT/UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Direct products Rg3, Rd, and liquiritin-2′-O-glucoside are experimentally shown.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Rg3/Rd are ginsenosides (triterpenoid saponins) and liquiritin-2′-O-glucoside is a flavonoid glycoside; this follows from substrates/products described.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Section 2.7 explicitly says Rg3 is a rare ginsenoside and a primary component of traditional Chinese antitumor drugs; the paper also notes health benefits of ginsenosides/flavonoids.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Methods explicitly describe heterologous expression in E. coli Rosetta (DE3) for protein production.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Methods specify in vitro assays with UDP-Glc at 37 °C and crystallization/co-crystallization with Rh2 or F2.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Section 2.7 states wild type is ≈4-fold more efficient toward F2 than Rh2 and that many combined mutations reduced Rh2 catalytic efficiency.

Other_Important_Info.reason: PDB accessions 8JZQ, 8K08, 8K09 are given in Data Availability; Results support Nα5-oriented pocket, linker role, hydrophobic Rh2 recognition, F2-specific hydrogen-bond contribution, limited promiscuity, and liquiritin glycosylation.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each numbered point is directly supported by Results sections 2.1-2.8 and Table 1.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper explicitly discusses enzyme engineering/biomanufacturing value for Rg3, especially S175A/L181W for in vitro production; this is not a plant breeding study.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The double mutant is derived from Pq3-O-UGT2, whose accession ALE15280.1 is given, but the exact phrasing 'Derived from GenBank: ALE15280.1' is not stated in the paper.

Enzyme_Name.reason: This is a faithful descriptive name for the engineered Pq3-O-UGT2 double mutant generated by site-directed mutagenesis.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The mutant acts in the same ginsenoside glycosylation/triterpenoid saponin pathway as the wild type.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results specifically emphasize enhanced/selective conversion in the Rh2→Rg3 3-O-glycan extension branch.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Section 2.7 and Table 1 show the engineered variant has greater catalytic efficiency toward Rh2 and lower efficiency toward F2 than wild type/L181W, i.e. more selective for Rh2 glycosylation.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is explicitly stated for the mutant or the enzyme in the paper.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Kinetic analyses in Table 1 test UDP-Glc as donor with Rh2 and F2 as acceptors for this mutant.

Primary_Product.reason: The mutant acts on the same reactions as wild type, producing Rg3 from Rh2 and Rd from F2.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Table 1/Section 2.7 support direct product formation and shifted substrate preference toward Rh2/Rg3 production.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: As with wild type, this is a terminal glycosylation/decorative step on preformed ginsenosides.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The mutant is highlighted as useful for selective Rg3 production, but the paper does not establish it as a rate-limiting or control step in vivo.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Table 1 gives 126.40 ± 2.93 s−1 mM−1 for Rh2, 4.43-fold WT, and 54.29 ± 2.05 s−1 mM−1 for F2; text says Rh2 efficiency is ≈2-fold greater than F2.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Section 2.7/Methods support site-directed mutagenesis, purified enzyme assays, Michaelis-Menten kinetics, and HPLC quantification.

Source_Species.reason: As for the parent gene, the provided text does not explicitly state Panax quinquefolius as the source species.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The exact Latin species name is not explicitly given in the provided paper text for this mutant.

Applied_Species.reason: Mutants were expressed in E. coli under the same protocol as wild type.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods specify E. coli Rosetta (DE3), i.e. Escherichia coli.

Applied_Promoters.reason: pET-28a is stated, but the paper does not explicitly name the T7 promoter in the provided text.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: As a mutant of Pq3-O-UGT2, it remains a plant family 1 UGT with GT-B fold and PSPG motif; the parent enzyme is characterized as such.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The mutant's emphasized target product is Rg3 from Rh2, directly supported by Section 2.7.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Section 2.7 explicitly describes Rg3 as a rare ginsenoside and a primary component of traditional Chinese antitumor drugs.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Mutants were heterologously expressed in E. coli Rosetta (DE3) using the same protocol as wild type.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Methods specify in vitro kinetics at 37 °C with UDP-Glc.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Section 2.7 states broader multi-site combinations often reduced Rh2 activity compared to individual mutations.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Section 2.7 states Ser175 and Leu181 are near the N-terminal end of Nα6 and highlights this mutant as promising for Rg3 production.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly supported by Section 2.7 and Table 1.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion explicitly says the engineered enzyme has great potential for in vitro Rg3 production and suggests thermostability improvement and immobilization strategies.
